commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=03165abd3152fa557a8d0626bc9bbd52e0e20526)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Amazon Bedrock memory credential probing to skip the AWS SDK chain unless selected AWS signals are present, temporarily disables IMDS during some probes, memoizes process-env probe results, and adds unit coverage.

PR surface: Source +92, Tests +76. Total +168 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible: current main and v2026.6.1 call the AWS SDK defaultProvider after static credential checks, and dependency source shows that chain can reach IMDS. I did not run the reported NemoClaw/OpenShell sandbox path, so real-environment reproduction remains unproven here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose the Bedrock IMDS/default-chain compatibility policy and require real after-fix proof before this external PR can merge.

target.title: fix(amazon-bedrock): skip IMDS during credential auto-detection (#64891)
target.body:
## Summary

- Stop Bedrock memory auto-detection from probing AWS IMDS (`169.254.169.254`) when no AWS credential signals are configured.
- During profile/container credential chain probes, temporarily set `AWS_EC2_METADATA_DISABLED=true` so `defaultProvider()` skips instance metadata while still resolving profiles and container roles.
- Memoize `process.env` credential probes for 5 minutes and coalesce concurrent calls to avoid startup bursts.

Closes #64891

## Real behavior proof

With no AWS env vars and a mocked credential loader, `hasAwsCredentials()` returns `false` without loading the SDK:

```powershell
pnpm exec tsx -e "(async()=>{const { hasAwsCredentials } = await import('./extensions/amazon-bedrock/embedding-provider.ts'); const calls=[]; const loader=async()=>{calls.push('loaded'); return { defaultProvider:()=>async()=>({accessKeyId:'should-not-run'})};}; const empty=await hasAwsCredentials({}, loader); const disabled=await hasAwsCredentials({AWS_EC2_METADATA_DISABLED:'true'}, loader); console.log(JSON.stringify({emptyEnv:empty,disabledEnv:disabled,credentialProviderLoads:calls.length},null,2));})();"
```

Output:

```json
{
  "emptyEnv": false,
  "disabledEnv": false,
  "credentialProviderLoads": 0
}
```

Before this change, the empty-env path loaded `@aws-sdk/credential-provider-node` and invoked `defaultProvider()`, which walked the chain to IMDS.

## Tests

- `pnpm test extensions/amazon-bedrock/embedding-provider.test.ts`

## Test plan

- [x] Empty env skips credential SDK load
- [x] `AWS_EC2_METADATA_DISABLED=true` exits without SDK when no probe signals
- [x] `AWS_PROFILE` probe disables IMDS temporarily and restores env
- [x] `process.env` probes are memoized and coalesced
- [x] `AWS_ENABLE_IMDS=true` opts into full chain probing
- [ ] Maintainer: non-AWS sandbox startup shows no repeated `169.254.169.254` traffic
target.author: ly85206559
